_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize AK state Senate batch: Merrick, Tobin, Hoffman, Claman, Cronk. Per-candidate cited research; party-line marquee votes + bill sponsorships + issue-group endorsements as HARD evidence; candidacy-currency fixes (Hoffman retiring, Claman running for governor, Merrick/Cronk seats not up till 2028/2029).

profile.confidence_note: Coalition/moderate Republican (Senate Majority Whip in the bipartisan majority; censured / no longer recognized by the AK Republican Party). Thin but cited record; seat not up until 2028 (won 4-yr term Nov 2024, term ends Jan 16 2029).

kelly-merrick.notes_append: AK Senate Dist L (Eagle River). Bipartisan-majority member; Freedom Index cumulative ~17%. Cross-party deviations: voted YES on SB240 (minors 16+ behavioral-health care without parental consent) and supported SB64 absentee-voting expansion. One pro-life data point (2022 vote to defund Medicaid abortion). Seat not on 2026 ballot.

4.note: Not on Planned Parenthood Alaska endorsement list; campaign funding is labor-union-dominated, no abortion-industry PAC money found.

1.note: Voted YES on SB240 (2024, 14-5), allowing minors 16+ to consent to behavioral/mental-health services and authorizing school personnel to recommend providers without parental consent.

3.note: As Senate Majority Whip in the 14-6 party-line majority that passed SB64 (2025), which expands absentee voting, ballot tracking, ballot curing, and prepaid return postage.
Voted the state-sovereignty position on HJR10 (2023) opposing federal National Park Service hunting restrictions in Alaska.

profile.confidence_note: Standard progressive Democrat; Senate Education Chair; running for re-election 2026 (primary Aug 18, 2026). Heavy HARD contrary record across life, marriage, family, immigration, and firearms. No cross-party conservative deviation found.

loki-tobin.notes_append: AK Senate Dist I (downtown Anchorage). Planned Parenthood-endorsed (2022). Prime sponsor of red-flag bill SB229 (gun violence protective orders + registry), SB203 (prohibited weapons), SB206 (firearm safe-storage grant fund). Co-sponsor comprehensive sex-ed SB43. Lead sponsor SB169 'Welcoming Alaska Office' for immigrants. Voted NO on safe-haven baby-box bill SB9 (2026).

0.note: Planned Parenthood-endorsed; argued against safe-haven baby-box bill SB9 — documented pro-abortion stance, no affirmation of personhood from conception.

1.note: Prime sponsor of contraceptive-coverage mandate SB27; PP-endorsed; voted no on safe-haven measure — no abortion-abolition advocacy, documented opposite.

4.note: Accepted Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates endorsement (2022).

0.note: Documented LGBTQ-advocacy record; publicly opposed the Governor's parental-rights bill as 'discrimination' — no affirmation of one-man/one-woman marriage.

1.note: Consistent LGBTQ-rights advocacy; no opposition to same-sex marriage/civil unions.

2.note: Opposed the parental-rights bill restricting gender content, saying 'I don't need to hear people justify discrimination' — does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Co-sponsor of SB43 comprehensive 'health and personal safety' (sex-ed) curriculum for public schools.

0.note: As Education Chair, advanced education bills adding homeschool reporting/testing/audit requirements, drawing homeschool-community opposition.

1.note: Publicly opposed the Governor's parental-rights/notification bill.

2.note: Co-sponsor of comprehensive sex-ed bill SB43.

3.note: Prime sponsor of SB229 framing firearm violence through 'gun violence protective orders' — a public-health/risk framing.

1.note: Prime sponsor of SB169 'Welcoming Alaska Office' to welcome immigrants — opposite of mandatory-deportation stance.

2.note: SB169 establishes a state immigrant-welcoming office — does not oppose sanctuary-type policies.

1.note: Prime sponsor of Alaska's red-flag bill SB229 (incl. a central registry for protective orders) and SB203 expanding prohibited-weapon definitions.

profile.confidence_note: Longest-serving AK legislator; rural conservative Democrat, Senate Finance Co-Chair. Announced June 25, 2025 he will NOT seek re-election in 2026 (retiring); holds seat through Jan 2027. Very thin social-issue record — most cells genuinely no-evidence.

lyman-hoffman.notes_append: AK Senate Dist S (Bethel / SW Alaska). Retiring 2026 (announced 2025-06-25). Documented votes: 1997 YEA on CSSB105 (parental consent before a minor's abortion); 2017 lone NAY (19-1) on SB54 voting to preserve the lenient SB91 criminal-justice regime. Career focused on rural fiscal issues (Power Cost Equalization), not social policy.

1.note: 1997 YEA on CSSB105 requiring parental consent before a minor's abortion (note: a dated vote, secondary-sourced; the only documented parental-consent data point).
In 2017 was the lone NAY (19-1) on SB54, voting to preserve the lenient SB91 criminal-justice-reform regime rather than restore tougher penalties.

set.office: State Senator (sitting · 2026 Democratic candidate for Governor)

profile.confidence_note: Sitting AK Senate Dist H; running for GOVERNOR in 2026 (not seeking Senate re-election; seat not on 2026 ballot). Senate Judiciary Chair. Strong progressive on life/marriage/guns, with a documented tough-on-crime cross-party credit as Judiciary chair.

matt-claman.notes_append: Running for Governor 2026 (announced Nov 2025; nonpartisan top-four primary Aug 18, 2026; running mate Sarah Skeel). Planned Parenthood-endorsed (2014 & 2024). Authored 2009 Anchorage LGBTQ anti-discrimination ordinance + 2018 state LGBTQ-protections bill. Authored HB75 red-flag bill (2018, NRA-opposed). Ranked-choice-voting champion. CROSS-PARTY CREDIT: led the 2024 omnibus crime bill (drug-dealer 2nd-degree murder; victim protections), signed by R Gov. Dunleavy.

0.note: Long-record abortion-rights advocate centering the state constitution's privacy right; no personhood affirmation.

1.note: Planned Parenthood-endorsed; documented pro-abortion record — no abolition advocacy.

4.note: Planned Parenthood-endorsed in 2014 and 2024 and personally volunteered for Planned Parenthood of Alaska.

0.note: Long-time LGBTQ-rights advocate; no support for one-man/one-woman marriage definition.

1.note: As Acting Mayor (2009) oversaw Anchorage's LGBTQ anti-discrimination ordinance; 2018 pushed a state LGBTQ-protections bill.

2.note: Documented LGBTQ-agenda advocacy; no affirmation of immutable biological sex.

4.note: Sponsored/championed LGBTQ anti-discrimination law (2009 Anchorage ordinance; 2018 state bill).

1.note: His LGBTQ anti-discrimination measures penalize businesses/nonprofits that decline service on conscience grounds — opposite of conscience exemptions.

2.note: Vocal champion of Alaska's ranked-choice voting / open-primary model — opposite of single-day in-person voting.

0.note: As Judiciary Chair led the 2024 omnibus crime bill and reports active involvement in 21 public-safety bills; signed by R Gov. Dunleavy — affirmative pro-public-safety leadership.

1.note: 2024 crime bill allows 2nd-degree murder charges for drug dealers in overdose deaths; also sponsoring SB155/SB265 — anti-soft-on-crime direction.

2.note: 2024 crime bill protects sexual-assault survivors and increases penalties for fatal drug dealing — victim-protective/proportional.

1.note: As House Judiciary chair (2018) authored HB75, a red-flag 'gun violence protective order' bill allowing court-ordered firearm seizure; NRA-opposed.

profile.confidence_note: Conservative Republican; elected Senate Minority Leader Dec 2025. Seat not up until 2029 (term ends Jan 16, 2029). Prior on-disk score (97) rested on uncited TRUEs; this pass re-bases on cited evidence only (8 documented TRUE cells; remaining cells nulled for lack of sourcing).

mike-cronk.notes_append: AK Senate Dist R (Tok/Interior); Senate Minority Leader. Cited record: prime sponsor SB136 firearm financial-privacy (NRA-thanked); voted against HB99 (adding SOGI to nondiscrimination law); 2021 & 2022 votes to defund Medicaid abortion (per Planned Parenthood opposition listing); op-ed championing homeschool/charter school choice. PP lists him on its opposition (anti-abortion) page. No church affiliation documented.

0.note: Planned Parenthood lists him as an anti-abortion candidate it opposes; voted (2021 & 2022) to eliminate Medicaid funding for abortion — documented pro-life record.

4.note: On Planned Parenthood's opposition list; no abortion-industry funding/endorsement found.

2.note: Voted AGAINST HB99, which would have added sexual-orientation/gender-identity to nondiscrimination law.

4.note: HB99 'no' vote — opposed expanding LGBTQ identity categories in public law.

0.note: Authored an op-ed defending homeschool/correspondence families and championing charter access and educational choice against HB69.

2.note: Prime sponsor of SB136 barring firearm-specific merchant-category codes — resisting federal/financial-sector gun-owner tracking; NRA-ILA publicly thanked him.

1.note: Prime sponsor of SB136, barring firearm merchant-category codes used to build de facto gun-owner registries; passed Senate committee 2025.

4.note: SB136 and accompanying advocacy oppose federal/financial-sector firearm surveillance of gun owners.
